summary:
The statement about effectiveness for hypertensive patients based solely on a p-value less than 0.1 does not confirm high reproducibility; this threshold only suggests an approximate 90% likelihood against randomness but doesn't guarantee real-world benefits nor precise success percentages per patient group. To validate such significant medical advancements rigorously requires further robust studies ensuring consistent outcomes across multiple trials under controlled conditions before widespread application can reasonably proceed without reservations over potential false positives arising purely through stochastic variation alone at given thresholds like those indicated herein via reported statistics. ```plaintext
A low p-value (< 0.10) implies that while the result may seem statistically significant, it still leaves room for up to 10% possibility that any apparent benefit occurred by chance. Further rigorous validation including larger sample sizes and replication are necessary to ensure reliable therapeutic effects beyond initial promising findings.
```
